title: Creating a new role prevents "Transfer the files" and "View Download Details" from working - with the Roles.php file fixed
author: nicolaselt
body:
### Description

When creating a custom (non-system) user role and enabling certain permissions in the "Manage Permissions" screen (`role-permissions.php`), some specific permissions are silently dropped when the form is saved. The checkbox appears checked in the UI, the browser correctly submits it in the POST request, but after the redirect the permission is not saved — the checkbox is unchecked again, with no error message anywhere (no PHP error, no JS console error, no failed network request).

Confirmed affected permissions: `upload` and `view_downloads_details`. There may be others affected depending on which legacy rows exist in the database (see root cause below) — any permission key that has a leftover row from the pre-custom-roles system with `role_level = 0` and `role_id = NULL` will be affected.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Enable custom roles (Settings > Advanced).
2. Create a new custom role (not "Client", not one of the default system roles).
3. Go to that role's permissions page (`role-permissions.php?role=X`).
4. Check the "Upload files" (`upload`) permission checkbox.
5. Click Save.
6. Reload the permissions page: the "Upload files" checkbox is unchecked again.

### Expected Behavior

 .

### Actual Behavior

 .

### ProjectSend Version

r2029

### PHP Version

8.2.31

### Database Type

MariaDB

### Database Version

11.4.12

### Web Server

Apache

### Browser

Firefox

### Relevant Log Output

```shell

```

### Screenshots

[github_issue_role_permissions_bug.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29677119/github_issue_role_permissions_bug.md)

[Roles.php](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29677162/Roles.php)

title: Download Link Token Changes After Copying the URL
author: 4allprograms
body:
### Description

Hello,

I've noticed something unusual with ProjectSend download links.

When I open a download page, I can see a specific token in the URL. However, after I copy the same URL and paste it into a text editor or browser address bar, I realize that the token is different from the one I originally saw.

I was expecting the token to remain the same, since it appears to be the same file and the same download link.

Is this normal behavior in ProjectSend? Does the system automatically regenerate tokens, or could there be another component (such as a plugin, custom code, or redirect) modifying the URL?

Any clarification would be appreciated. Thank you.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Log in to ProjectSend as an administrator.
2. Open a file that has a public download link.
3. Click the public download link and note the token shown in the URL.
4. Copy the URL from the browser address bar.
5. Paste the URL into a text editor or a new browser tab.
6. Compare the token in the pasted URL with the token originally displayed.
7. Notice that the token is different.

### Expected Behavior

The download URL token should remain constant for the same file. When the token changes unexpectedly, shared download links may become invalid and users can receive a 403 Forbidden error when attempting to access or download the file. The expected behavior is that the original download link continues to work without generating a different token.

### Actual Behavior

The token in the download URL changes unexpectedly. The token displayed when the link is first opened is different from the token obtained after copying the URL. Because of this mismatch, some download requests fail with a 403 Forbidden error, even though the file is public and the original link should be valid.

### ProjectSend Version

R2029

### PHP Version

8.3.31

### Database Type

MariaDB

### Database Version

10.6.27

### Web Server

Nginx 1.30.2

### Browser

chrome, firefox

### Relevant Log Output

```shell

```

### Screenshots

_No response_

title: No rights to upload files after upgrade to r2029
author: quintingsnj
body:
### Description

Cannot change "upload files" and "view download details". Adding a checkmark and saving, item becomes unchecked. Users in uploader role cannot upload files.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to user Roles, edit uploader
2. Check off upload files and click save
3. Look at upload files - checkmark is gone.
4. I also tried creating a new role, and the upload files checkmark does not stay checked.

### Expected Behavior

Upload files checkmark should stay there. 

### Actual Behavior

Expected behaviour would be that the checkmark for "upload files" would remain checked.

### ProjectSend Version

r2029

### PHP Version

8.4.10

### Database Type

MySQL

### Database Version

MySQL 8.0.46

### Web Server

Apache 2.4.52

### Browser

Chrome 149.0.7827.104 

### Relevant Log Output

```shell

```

### Screenshots

I was able to temporarily fix things by going into the DB...I found an entry for role level 0 upload, but it was tied to role id 1 - I changed the id to 2, and now it works (I don't use role id 1 (client). I did try adding one, but wasn't sure what the role level should be...the level is 0 for both client and uploader (0 and 2), and I could not add an upload role on level 0 because it existed - so I just modified the existing one to be role id 2....

title: Large Image files and hard coded php memory_limit crashes site
author: chico11mbit
body:
### Description

Hi,
when uploading large image files over 512MB (JPG,TIF, PSD etc.) the site crashes after upload (white page).
It crashed also when a large JPG with more than 40K pixel width is uploaded and it is under the memory_limit.

It seems that this is connected to the hardcoded memory limit 512MB in /config/php/projectsend.ini
I think the system is trying to create thumbnails from the uploaded files and crashes because of the filesize and the memory limit.

create large tif over 512MB
Upload it

title: Encryption settings not working
author: Marvin36
body: ### Description Files get encrypted regardless of maximum encryption file size or user selection on the upload page. This happens even though mandatory encryption is deactivated. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Activate "Enable file encryption feature" in Encryption Settings 2. **Deactivate** "Make encryption mandatory" 3. Set "Maximum file size for encryption (MB)" to any value 4. Add a file that is larger than the given value 5. Deselect "Encrypt files on server" in the upload screen 6. Upload file --- --- ### Expected Behavior The file does not get encrypted. ### Actual Behavior The file gets encrypted regardless of the chosen settings. Since the maximum encryption size is not working as intended, this can also lead to memory exhaustion when it tries to encrypt larger files, depending on the memory_limit. Example for a 350 MB file with memory_limit at 256M: ### ProjectSend Version r2029 ### PHP Version 8.3 ### Database Type MySQL ### Database Version _No response_ ### Web Server _No response_ ### Browser _No response_ ### Relevant Log Output ```shell ``` ### Screenshots _No response_

title: Bug: `/upload.php` widget fails to load when Content Security Policy is enabled
author: glego
body: ## Background Security scanners such as `https://securityheaders.com/` check whether a website has important security headers enabled. If security headers like `Content-Security-Policy`, `X-Frame-Options`, `X-Content-Type-Options`, `Referrer-Policy`, or `Permissions-Policy` are missing, the website security rating is capped or reduced. Example of currently missing or weak security headers that can negatively affect the rating: ```http Content-Security-Policy: missing X-Frame-Options: missing X-Content-Type-Options: missing Referrer-Policy: missing Permissions-Policy: missing ``` In this case, security headers are being added via the Kubernetes ingress and injected at the edge/proxy layer. Therefore, this may be more of a **feature request / compatibility issue** than a classic bug: the application should support running correctly when modern security headers, especially CSP, are enabled. ## Description ## Affected Page * Confirm that Content Security Policy is enabled. * `https://example.domain/upload.php` ## Summary When opening `/upload.php`, the upload/widget area does not load correctly. Instead, the page displays the following fallback/error message: ```text Your browser doesn't support HTML5, Flash or Silverlight. Please update your browser or install Adobe Flash or Silverlight to continue. ``` The browser console shows multiple errors related to Content Security Policy and a blocked CKEditor script. ## Current Behavior The widget on `/upload.php` does not load. The browser console reports: ```text Refused to execute script from 'https://example.domain//node_modules/@ckeditor/ckeditor5-build-classic/build/ckeditor.js?v=23.1.0' because its MIME type ('text/html') is not executable, and strict MIME type checking is enabled. ``` Additional CSP violation: ```text Executing inline script violates the following Content Security Policy directive: "script-src 'self' ..." Either the 'unsafe-inline' keyword, a hash ('sha256-kyFFyoMkFsRr2mzvVv8VxB/0uuCeekHS3HT8iYQLk3M='), or a nonce ('nonce-...') is required to enable inline execution. ``` ## Console Output ```text Refused to execute script from 'https://example.domain//node_modules/@ckeditor/ckeditor5-build-classic/build/ckeditor.js?v=23.1.0' because its MIME type ('text/html') is not executable, and strict MIME type checking is enabled. upload.php:307 Executing inline script violates the following Content Security Policy directive: "script-src 'self' ...". Either the 'unsafe-inline' keyword, a hash ('sha256-kyFFyoMkFsRr2mzvVv8VxB/0uuCeekHS3HT8iYQLk3M='), or a nonce ('nonce-...') is required to enable inline execution. The action has been blocked. ``` ## Suspected Cause There appear to be multiple related issues. ### 1. CKEditor script URL may be incorrect The script is requested from: ```text https://example.domain//node_modules/@ckeditor/ckeditor5-build-classic/build/ckeditor.js?v=23.1.0 ``` The **double slash** after the domain may indicate that the generated asset path is wrong: ```text example.domain//node_modules/... ``` The server appears to return an HTML page instead of the JavaScript file, causing this browser error: ```text MIME type ('text/html') is not executable ``` This could mean one of the following: - The CKEditor file is missing. - The path is wrong. - The request is redirected to an HTML error page. - The request is redirected to a login page. - The server is not serving `.js` files with the correct MIME type. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Open: ```text https://example.domain/upload.php ``` 2. Confirm that Content Security Policy is enabled. 3. Observe that the upload/widget component does not load. 4. Open the browser developer console. 5. Check for blocked script and CSP errors. ## Relevant CSP Header ```http Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; base-uri 'self'; object-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'self'; form-action 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; script-src 'self' 'sha256-vOlV5sV2qalDz8p2djblMJqtWiCukAxnb+eoru3ThK0=' 'sha256-czOVuEgQH7KHmIS7/hz0INIy/iJaEYmR7vUzc9q+WIs=' 'sha256-1Hra4JvtaVFPYaBKjn0j8rRAtnNyAbO4SEQCrZpzhL4=' 'sha256-ncc1YrJI6H9OzrnbYnel044BA+U+qbqvw14txUV4ryk=' 'sha256-DoiRYRbHXM1RuZdqgVP6M4WDgGnBstNQrVoU4pUffpk=' 'sha256-+ZM9J2J4Sqkz+mu44CxlE7W+GOC3Y8e2mQtMkhT2vsk=' 'sha256-EBWhGzSodDvER2JOT8jbEnZ9D+YMiGlmivH3MEC6Zsk='; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; upgrade-insecure-requests; ``` ### Expected Behavior ## Expected Behavior The upload/widget component should load successfully on `/upload.php` without CSP violations or blocked scripts. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open: ```text https://example.domain/upload.php ``` 2. Confirm that Content Security Policy is enabled. 3. Observe that the upload/widget component does not load. 4. Open the browser developer console. 5. Check for blocked script and CSP errors. ## Relevant CSP Header ```http Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; base-uri 'self'; object-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'self'; form-action 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; script-src 'self' 'sha256-vOlV5sV2qalDz8p2djblMJqtWiCukAxnb+eoru3ThK0=' 'sha256-czOVuEgQH7KHmIS7/hz0INIy/iJaEYmR7vUzc9q+WIs=' 'sha256-1Hra4JvtaVFPYaBKjn0j8rRAtnNyAbO4SEQCrZpzhL4=' 'sha256-ncc1YrJI6H9OzrnbYnel044BA+U+qbqvw14txUV4ryk=' 'sha256-DoiRYRbHXM1RuZdqgVP6M4WDgGnBstNQrVoU4pUffpk=' 'sha256-+ZM9J2J4Sqkz+mu44CxlE7W+GOC3Y8e2mQtMkhT2vsk=' 'sha256-EBWhGzSodDvER2JOT8jbEnZ9D+YMiGlmivH3MEC6Zsk='; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; upgrade-insecure-requests; ``` ### Actual Behavior ## Console Output ```text Refused to execute script from 'https://example.domain//node_modules/@ckeditor/ckeditor5-build-classic/build/ckeditor.js?v=23.1.0' because its MIME type ('text/html') is not executable, and strict MIME type checking is enabled. upload.php:307 Executing inline script violates the following Content Security Policy directive: "script-src 'self' ...". Either the 'unsafe-inline' keyword, a hash ('sha256-kyFFyoMkFsRr2mzvVv8VxB/0uuCeekHS3HT8iYQLk3M='), or a nonce ('nonce-...') is required to enable inline execution. The action has been blocked. ``` ### ProjectSend Version r2029 ### PHP Version _No response_ ### Database Type None ### Database Version _No response_ ### Web Server _No response_ ### Browser _No response_ ### Relevant Log Output ```shell ``` ### Screenshots _No response_

title: Activity log shows some 'logged in' and 'assigned file to client' events without author and client name
author: Dalbasar
body:
### Description

Most of the events in our activity log (including the login and 'assigned file to client') have a proper 'Author' name set and for client file assignment show the client's name. However at least since r1945 (and still in r2029), we observe activities without Author name (logged in event) and file assignments without 'Author', filename and  client name in the 'to client:' entry.

The 'logged in to the system' messages without an author do not appear in the list when filtering for the "Account logs in through the form" activity.
It seems like the issue is related to the 'Remember me function', so it might also depend on the clients browser configuration?


### Steps to Reproduce

1. Use the 'Remember me for 30 days' option on the login page
2. Try to trigger a login via the remember me token and assign a file to aclient.
3. Check Tools -> Actions -> Log and filter activities for 'A file is assigned to a client' or look for log in events
### Expected Behavior

- Activity author is always set when the activity can only be done by logged in users
- file assignments to clients should always contain the client's name in the 'to the client:' entry

### Actual Behavior

- Some activities are missing the author or other meta data

### ProjectSend Version

r2029

### PHP Version

8.4.16

### Database Type

MariaDB

### Database Version

MariaDB 11.8.6

### Web Server

nginx 1.28.3

### Browser

Firefox 140 ESR, Chrome 147

### Relevant Log Output

```shell
25/04/2026," assigned the file  to client ",,,,"Remember me token used with different user agent"
25/04/2026," logged in to the system",,,,"Remember me token validated and rotated"
....
21/04/2026," logged in to the system",,,,"Remember me token created"
```

title: PDF Rendering Engine Failure - Missing Content and Alpha Channel Corruption (v2029)
author: iincitr
body: ### Description Critical Bug: PDF Rendering Engine Failure - Missing Content and Alpha Channel Corruption (v2029) Hi, I am writing to report a fundamental failure in the PDF preview and processing engine of ProjectSend v2029. This is not just a minor transparency issue; the engine is consistently failing to render the full content of the documents. Detailed Problem Description: Missing Data: Large sections of the PDF—including text, table lines, and graphical elements—are completely missing or rendered as blank spaces in the preview. Transparency Corruption: Transparent elements (logos, stamps) are appearing as solid dark blocks (Alpha channel flattening failure). Download Corruption via Preview: When downloading through the process.php?do=serve_file endpoint (from the preview window), the resulting PDF remains corrupted with these missing elements and black boxes. Original File Integrity: The original files stored in /upload/files/ are perfectly fine. The corruption only happens when the system's internal engine (Imagick/Ghostscript) attempts to process or "serve" the file. Technical Context for v2029: It appears the current implementation of the rendering library is dropping vector layers and failing to composite the alpha channel against a white background. For a system handling official documents and certificates, this loss of data integrity is a critical blocker. Required Actions: Update the rendering logic to ensure all PDF layers are captured. Implement an explicit flattening to a white background for all previews. Ensure the "Download" function in the preview window serves the unprocessed original binary rather than a re-encoded stream. ### Steps to Reproduce Upload: Log-in to the ProjectSend v2029 dashboard and upload a high-quality PDF containing vector graphics, tables, and transparent elements (e.g., a corporate certificate with alpha-channel logos). Verify Original: Download the file immediately via the "Files" list using the standard download action or check it directly in the /upload/files/ directory. (Observe that the file is intact and correct). Trigger Preview: Click the "Preview" button for the same file to open the preview modal (invoking process.php?do=get_preview). Observe Rendering: Look at the rendered document in the preview window. (Observe missing text/lines and black/teal boxes replacing transparent logos). Download via Preview: While the preview modal is open, click the "Download" icon located in the preview toolbar (invoking process.php?do=serve_file). Compare: Open the file downloaded in step 5. (Observe that this file is now permanently corrupted with missing data and black boxes, unlike the original file from step 2). ### Expected Behavior Full Content Rendering: The preview engine should accurately render all layers of the PDF, including vector lines, text blocks, and tables, without dropping any data. Proper Compositing: Transparent elements (Alpha channels) should be automatically composited against a solid white background. Previews should look identical to the original document, with no black or dark boxes over logos. Raw File Serving: When a user clicks the "Download" icon within the preview interface, the system should serve the raw, unprocessed original binary from the storage directory. It should not be re-processed or filtered through an image library, ensuring the downloaded file remains 100% identical to the source. Consistency: The visual output in the preview modal must maintain the professional integrity of the document, as these are official certificates where data loss (missing text) is a critical failure. ### Actual Behavior Data Loss during Conversion: The PDF-to-image rendering engine (SimpleImage/Imagick) is failing to capture multiple vector layers. As a result, critical information such as table borders, text segments, and background graphics are omitted from the preview. Alpha Channel Defaulting to Black: Transparent regions in the original PDF are being rendered as solid dark or teal blocks. The engine is not flattening the alpha channel against a white background, which obscures logos and stamps. Stream Corruption: When downloading a file via process.php?do=serve_file (from the preview toolbar), the system delivers a processed, flattened version of the document that carries over the visual bugs from the preview. The downloaded file becomes unusable and visually different from the source file. Inconsistency with Storage: There is a direct contradiction between the healthy original file in the /upload/files/ directory and the corrupted output served by the application's preview logic. ### ProjectSend Version r2029 ### PHP Version 8.3.26 ### Database Type MariaDB ### Database Version 10.3.39 ### Web Server apache 2.4.57 ### Browser chorome , edge ### Relevant Log Output ```shell Following images preview and orj. file ``` ### Screenshots

title: A user can see another user's folder
author: lstep
body:
### Description

A user (system user, defined as role Uploader), should not be able to see folders created by other Uploaders. The files created in that folder are not visible, but the folder itself is visible.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create two system users, with the role "Uploader", without changing anything in the role definition
2. User 1 creates a folder "Foobar" and adds some files into it (not important)
3. User 2 logs in, and can see that there is a folder "Foobar" even thought this folder was created by User 1.

### Expected Behavior

Uploaders should not be able to see other's folders, like they can't see other's files.


### Actual Behavior

Any user can see other's folders names.

### ProjectSend Version

r2029

### PHP Version

8.3.6

### Database Type

None

### Database Version

_No response_

### Web Server

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

title: Feature request: Generic OIDC provider support (Socal Login)
author: glego
body: ### Description ProjectSend already supports providers like Google and Facebook, ect.. but support for a generic OIDC provider configuration would be a major improvement. This would allow integration with platforms like Keycloak, Authentik, and Authelia without requiring provider-specific implementations. Generic OIDC support would make authentication far more flexible for self-hosters. Something like nexcloud, which also supports custom OIDC https://apps.nextcloud.com/apps/sociallogin ### Use Case Some users use a central identity provider for applications such as Keycloak, Authentik, or Authelia. They want ProjectSend to authenticate against the same OIDC provider so they can sign in with existing accounts, benefit from SSO and MFA, and avoid managing separate credentials for file exchange. ### Alternatives Considered Yes, nothing found ### Additional Context _No response_

title: Editing a client requires setting Max. upload size
author: DominiqueCEOS
body:
### Description

When changing client settings, clicking on Save client fails with "This field is required." for the Max upload filesize parameter. 

Workaround: Always set a maximum filesize when editing a client (can be set to 0 for system defaults).

Note a big issue here, but could be a bit inconvenient if someone has edit multiple clients.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Edit a client that uses the default system limit for Max. upload size
2. Change some setting (other than Max upload filesize)
3. Save client

### Expected Behavior
- Edit client dialog shows User updated successfully and settings get applied

### Actual Behavior

- Edit client dialog shows "This field is required" next to Max. upload filesize and the settings don't get applied


### ProjectSend Version

r2029

### PHP Version

8.4.16

### Database Type

MariaDB

### Database Version

MariaDB 11.8.6

### Web Server

nginx 1.28.3

### Browser

Firefox 140.9.0 ESR

### Relevant Log Output

```shell

```

### Screenshots

_No response_

title: Changing client active status seems to fail with 'Internal Server Error 500' via Edit client dialog
author: DominiqueCEOS
body:
### Description

When setting the active status of a client (created with r2029?) via Edit client dialog, the appears to fail with an Internal Server Error (500) shown as a browser message with retry option (Firefox). Retry succeeds. Even without retrying, the requested changes were applied.
Not a big issue since the changes get applied, but it might be confusing to account managers. I didn't observe this behavior with an old client from some pre-R1720  version.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Log in as system administrator or account manager 
2. Create a new client (on some exisiting clients i didn't observe the issue?)
3. Set the client inactive via the Edit client dialog (it does not matter whether this is done after the client had already logged in)
4. Set a max upload file size (required due to bug in r2029, see #1560 )
5. Save client

### Expected Behavior

- Success message in Edit client dialog: User updated successfully.

### Actual Behavior

- ErrorCode: 500 Internal Server Error (browser message with retry option)
- Retry -> Confirm -> Expected success message in Edit client dialog.
- Note: Even without retry, the settings have already been applied 

### ProjectSend Version

r2029

### PHP Version

8.4.16

### Database Type

MariaDB

### Database Version

MariaDB 11.8.6

### Web Server

nginx 1.28.3

### Browser

Firefox 140.9.0 ESR

### Relevant Log Output

```shell

```

### Screenshots

_No response_
